Title: GraphQL Error Handling — Invalid Queries Return Structured Errors
Preconditions: Runner-native (scripts/graphql-runner.ts). Server schema introspection cached; runner validates client-side before send, so deliberately-broken queries surface schema-validation errors (DV-006/DV-009/DV-SYNTAX) in errors[] with data=null — mirrors the structured GraphQL-error contract expected from the server side.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=unknown_field] errors[] non-empty — unknown field on CustomerOrderType (DV-009) (BL-GQL-001)
[DATA label=unknown_field] data is null
[ERRORS label=unknown_root] errors[] non-empty — unknown root query field (DV-006) (BL-GQL-001)
[DATA label=unknown_root] data is null
[ERRORS label=missing_args] errors[] non-empty — addItem requires command argument (DV-008/DV-010) (BL-GQL-001)
[DATA label=missing_args] data is null
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] Error messages reference field names by name (INVALID_FIELD, nonExistentTopLevelField, command) — verify in evidence JSON
[EVIDENCE] No stack traces, internal paths, connection strings, or SQL hints in error messages (BL-GQL-001)
Failure_Signals: errors[] empty on obviously invalid query; error messages expose internal details (stack traces, file paths); crash instead of structured error response
References: xAPI; graphql-schema.md — error contract & DV-* codes

Title: GraphQL Authentication — Auth Gating On Protected Operations
Preconditions: Runner-native. Runs public categories query (STORE_ID from env), then me/orders without token (xAPI does not 401 anonymous — returns empty data), then acquires ORG_USER token and re-runs to confirm the same resolvers now return real member data. Demonstrates resolver-level auth gating (BL-GQL-004).
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=public_cats] errors[] empty — categories query is public (no auth required) when storeId provided (BL-GQL-004)
[DATA label=public_cats] data.categories is non-null
[ERRORS label=noauth_me] errors[] empty — xAPI allows anonymous me() but resolver returns null member
[DATA label=noauth_me] data.me.memberId is null — anonymous caller has no member (BL-GQL-004)
[ERRORS label=noauth_orders] errors[] non-empty — orders() is hard-gated: returns Unauthorized extension code (BL-GQL-004)
[ERRORS label=auth_me] errors[] empty — same query succeeds with ORG_USER Bearer token
[DATA label=auth_me] data.me.memberId is non-null
[DATA label=auth_me] data.me.contact is non-null
[DATA label=auth_me] data.me.contact.id is non-null
[ERRORS label=auth_orders] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=auth_orders] data.orders.totalCount >= 0
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] noauth_me returns memberId:null (soft gate) — not leaking internal user IDs or stack traces (BL-GQL-004)
[EVIDENCE] noauth_orders error message extensions.code = "Unauthorized" (hard gate) — verify in evidence JSON
[EVIDENCE] auth_me memberId/contact.id differ from anonymous response — resolver uses Bearer token to scope response
Failure_Signals: Anonymous me() returns real member data; anonymous orders() returns data without Unauthorized error; authenticated me() returns null; error surfaces internal details (stack traces, SQL)

Title: GraphQL Query Performance — Happy-Path Response Times
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. Evidence JSON captures elapsed_ms per operation — performance targets are asserted as info notes (PERF tags) for manual/CI-side threshold eval; runner MVP has no TIMING assertion kind.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=perf_profile] errors[] empty — me resolves
[DATA label=perf_profile] data.me is non-null
[ERRORS label=perf_orders_list] errors[] empty — flat orders list resolves
[COUNT label=perf_orders_list] data.orders.totalCount >= 0
[ERRORS label=perf_orders_nested] errors[] empty — deep nested orders query resolves
[DATA label=perf_orders_nested] data.orders is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[PERF label=perf_profile] elapsed_ms target < 500ms (captured in evidence)
[PERF label=perf_orders_list] elapsed_ms target < 500ms (captured in evidence)
[PERF label=perf_orders_nested] elapsed_ms target < 1000ms (captured in evidence)
[PERF label=perf_orders_nested] N+1 detection (runner-v2): repeat 5x and verify elapsed_ms does not grow proportionally
Failure_Signals: Any query exceeds its target threshold; response times grow linearly with repetition (N+1); errors[] non-empty on happy-path query; timeout/5xx

Title: GraphQL Introspection — Schema Access Returns Valid Response
Preconditions: Runner-native. Public introspection query — no auth required. Runner's client-side validator supports introspection (validates against meta-schema). Asserts structured response + absence of sensitive type names.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=introspect] errors[] empty — introspection enabled on QA env (acceptable; production should disable) (BL-GQL-001)
[DATA label=introspect] data.__schema is non-null
[DATA label=introspect] data.__schema.queryType is non-null
[DATA label=introspect] data.__schema.queryType.name = Query
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] Scan data.__schema.types[].name for sensitive substrings "internal", "debug", "admin", "secret", "password" — evidence review
[PERF label=introspect] elapsed_ms target < 1000ms (full schema introspection is heavy)
[PERF] Rate-limiting spec (runner-v2): 10 rapid introspection queries — expected to throttle or return uniform data; not asserted in MVP
Failure_Signals: Introspection response missing __schema block; errors[] non-empty on plain introspection; sensitive internal types exposed; 5xx on introspection
